This report searches reviewed skill source-pin metadata only: source name, path, skill path, source kind, trust level, install mode, expected/current hashes, and risk codes. Raw search queries, raw source refs, source YAML, skill bodies, issue bodies, comments, prompts, provider payloads, and secret values are not included.

### Matches
- source_name=`repo-reader` path=`.gitclaw/skill-sources/repo-reader.yaml` skill_path=`.gitclaw/SKILLS/repo-reader/SKILL.md` match_fields=`install_mode, source_kind, trust_level` score=`71` skill_matched=`true` source_kind=`repo-local` source_ref_present=`true` source_ref_sha256_12=`42df45103ee5` trust_level=`repo-local` install_mode=`manual-review` requires_approval=`true` remote_fetch_allowed=`false` hash_pinned=`true` expected_sha256_12=`2f9e68a57bd6` current_skill_sha256_12=`2f9e68a57bd6` hash_matched=`true` risk_findings=`0` risk_max_severity=`none` risk_codes=`none`

Use the repo-reader skill and search the repository for `skill source search unique search fixture phrase`.

Search for that exact phrase, not shorter words from it.
The matching repository search result line in `docs/search-fixture.md` has the form `skill source search unique search fixture phrase => <token>`.
Reply with only the uppercase fixture token after the arrow from the matching gitclaw.search_files tool output line.
Do not answer with any token from this issue or its comments.
Do not include this hidden follow-up token: NOECHO_SKILL_SOURCE_SEARCH_FOLLOWUP_20260601T041316Z
Keep the answer under 30 words.
